title: sida rufescens
intro: Sida rufescens, also known as the rough-fruited sida, is a plant species belonging to the Malvaceae family, commonly found in tropical and subtropical regions. Traditionally, the plant has been used in various folk medicine practices due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Sida rufescens include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and treat various skin conditions. In herbal preparations, Sida rufescens is often used to make infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be consumed as teas or applied topically to the affected areas.
